desc: BadTV per-scanline horizontal displacement (waviness) field measured at t=0 through headless FCP via a vertical-line centroid probe. Validates the decode direction (MT19937 -> per-row random walk).
method: vertical bright line at x=960 on black, rendered through PAEBadTV (Static=0, Roll=0, Saturate=0, ScanBright=1) at t=0; per-row line-centroid x minus 960 = that row's horizontal displacement.

waviness_0.note: Waviness=0 => zero displacement (the deterministic scan-line-only exact path)

findings.structure: row-to-row displacement autocorrelation = 0.996 => a VERY SMOOTH 1-D RANDOM WALK per scanline, NOT white noise. Low-dimensional and MATCHABLE (unlike PAENoise's 2-D per-pixel white field).

decode_status: RNG identified as std::mt19937 (PAEGenerateNoise @ProAppsFxSupport 0xa783c, verified canonical constants). At t=0 the seed is fixed => the walk is byte-recoverable in principle. This probe confirms the field is a smooth low-dim random walk = the structure MT19937->cumulative-increment produces.
remaining_for_match: port MT19937 + the exact per-row increment draw + normalization + the Waviness->pixel-displacement scale + the HgcBadTV scanline-index sampling, then verify at t=0 via the spatial harness (BadTV is a FILTER = node-boundary testable).

generation_chain_decoded.1_seed: seed = (autoAnimate ? frameNum*0x3021(=12321) + randomSeed : randomSeed) XOR sign-mask(isMono). At t=0 frame=0 -> seed = randomSeed (^isMono mask).
generation_chain_decoded.2_mt19937_init: std::mt19937 seeded via the CANONICAL recurrence mt[0]=seed; mt[i]=(mt[i-1]^(mt[i-1]>>30))*1812433253(=0x6C078965)+i for i=1..623 (loop @0xa7a44, count 0x270=624).
generation_chain_decoded.3_permutation: a 256-entry identity byte array (strb i @0xa7a18) is std::shuffle'd via PCRandomShuffle @0xa857c = libc++ std::shuffle: for i=n-1..1 draw j=uniform_int_distribution(0,i)(mt), swap [i]<->[j].
generation_chain_decoded.4_bitmap: PCCreateBitmap(256,1,ChannelOrder=5,1) -> the shuffled perm becomes a 256x1 noise LUT (HSampleTiledNoise).
generation_chain_decoded.5_shader: HgcBadTV samples this 1-D LUT at a scanline-derived coord (floor(row-proj/P9.x)); the notes' normalize + 3-tap smooth yields the smooth per-row displacement * Waviness (measured autocorr 0.996).
generation_chain_decoded.bit_exact_caveat: steps 2-3 are libc++-implementation-specific: std::mt19937 is portable/canonical (exact), but std::uniform_int_distribution's rejection sampling AND std::shuffle's iteration direction are libc++-specific -- a byte-exact port must mirror libc++ (not just 'an' MT+shuffle). Decoded to identity; the port must replicate libc++ semantics.
generation_chain_decoded.status: FULL generation chain decoded to the algorithm+identity level. Remaining = a libc++-faithful port (mt19937 canonical + libc++ uniform_int_distribution + libc++ std::shuffle + HSampleTiledNoise tiling + 3-tap smooth + Waviness scale), then verify at t=0.

rng_full_spec.desc: Complete deterministic spec of PAEGenerateNoise NoiseType=0's noise field, decoded from ProAppsFxSupport @0xa783c/@0xa857c.
rng_full_spec.seed: seedBase = frameNum*0x3021 (12321) + randomSeed; seed = autoAnimate ? seedBase : randomSeed; then seed ^= (isMono ? -1 : 0) sign-extended mask. At t=0 frame 0 => seed = randomSeed (XOR mono mask).
rng_full_spec.mt19937: canonical std::mt19937: mt[0]=seed&0xffffffff; mt[i]=(1812433253*(mt[i-1]^(mt[i-1]>>30))+i)&0xffffffff for i=1..623; tempering u^=u>>11; u^=(u<<7)&0x9d2c5680; u^=(u<<15)&0xefc60000; u^=u>>18.
rng_full_spec.shuffle: libc++ std::shuffle FORWARD: identity byte array a[0..255]; for i=1..255: j=uniform_int_distribution(0,i)(mt); swap a[i],a[j]. (verified forward direction + growing range from the disasm loop @0xa85c4.)
rng_full_spec.fill: 256x1 RGBA bitmap: per pixel i, R,G = nested perm-hash lookups (perm[perm[..]+..]), B = i, A = from a SECOND re-seeded MT (madd w9*0x6c078965+.. then uniform draws). Then HSampleTiledNoise samples this tiled.
rng_full_spec.shader: HgcBadTV samples hg_Texture0 at a coord from ONE texCoord projection (floor(row/P9.x)) => 1-D per-scanline row index; 3-tap smoothed; * Waviness => horizontal displacement (measured smooth walk, autocorr 0.996).
rng_full_spec.caveat: libc++ uniform_int_distribution mask width must match libc++'s exact __independent_bits_engine formula for a bit-exact perm; validate the reproduced perm against a headless probe before trusting the full port.
rng_full_spec.mt19937_validated: MT19937 impl PROVEN bit-exact vs the canonical reference vector: std::mt19937(5489) first 5 outputs = [3499211612, 581869302, 3890346734, 3586334585, 545404204], matching Matsumoto mt19937ar exactly. The generator core is bit-exact.
rng_full_spec.libcxx_uniform: libc++ uniform_int_distribution<size_t>(0,b): Rp=b+1; w=(Rp-1).bit_length(); draw 32-bit MT words, take low w bits, REJECT values>=Rp. (standard libc++ __independent_bits_engine; single word since w<=8 for a 256-shuffle.) NOTE: could not run a libc++ reference binary to confirm the exact bit-extraction (macOS/navi sandbox SIGKILLs freshly-compiled binaries), so this stage is from libc++ source logic, not a live diff -- validate against a headless perm probe when porting.

rng_chain_decode.desc: Full RNG chain for PAEGenerateNoise NoiseType=0, decoded from ProAppsFxSupport 0xa783c+.
rng_chain_decode.seed: seed = autoAnimate ? (frameNum*0x3021 + randomSeed) : randomSeed, then XOR sbfx(isMono). 0x3021=12321. At t=0 frame=0 => seed=randomSeed (XOR isMono mask).
rng_chain_decode.mt19937: canonical MT19937: mt[0]=seed; mt[i]=(1812433253*(mt[i-1]^(mt[i-1]>>30))+i)&0xffffffff for i=1..623. (init multiplier 0x6C078965=1812433253 confirmed in disasm @0xa7a2c.)
rng_chain_decode.shuffle: libc++ std::shuffle FORWARD of a 256-entry identity byte array: for i=1..255: j=uniform_int_distribution(0,i)(mt); swap a[i],a[j]. (loop @0xa85a4 iterates first+1..last, range grows +1 each step.)
rng_chain_decode.lut_fill: 256x1 RGBA LUT: R=G=perm[perm[i]] (NESTED perm lookup, ldrb-ldrb @0xa7ab4/0xa7ac0), .z=counter, .w=alpha. Then a SECOND MT-reseeded loop (madd w9,w26,w8 @0xa7b24) draws more uniform_int values for a paired field.
rng_chain_decode.sampler: HSampleTiledNoise (0xb77xx) samples the LUT tiled; the HgcBadTV shader reads it at a SINGLE scanline-projected coord (floor(row/P9.x)) => 1-D per-row displacement, *Waviness, 3-tap smoothed (measured autocorr 0.996).
rng_chain_decode.status: RNG chain FULLY SPECIFIED + implementable. MT19937 + libc++ shuffle reproduced in /tmp/mt_shuffle.py (valid 256-perms). Remaining: pin libc++ uniform_int_distribution mask width exactly, port the nested-perm LUT + HSampleTiledNoise tiling + shader scanline sampling + 3-tap smooth, verify at t=0.

dsfmt_validation.desc: BadTV WAVINESS path VALIDATED against the already-verified dSFMT (NOT mt19937 -- that is the separate Static overlay). createWavyTableOfHeight @0x7df38 uses RandMersenne::SetSeed(2*frame+1) + dsfmt_gen_rand_all = the parity-VERIFIED dSFMT (curve.rng.dsfmt=0.0).
dsfmt_validation.seed: (uint)(2*frame)+1; at t=0 frame=0 => SEED=1 (confirmed @0x7dfa0: frameFromFxTime -> fadd d0,d0,d0 -> fcvtzu -> +1).
dsfmt_validation.walk: tmp[0]=draw0-1; tmp[i]=tmp[i-1]+(draw_i-1-0.5); scale=2*max|tmp|; norm=tmp/scale+0.5; 3-tap smooth; shader disp=(1-table)*Waviness (sign per roll direction).
dsfmt_validation.validation: Reproduced from dSFMT(seed=1): std=12.71 vs measured 12.76; range [-49.7,1.0] vs [-49.5,1.0]; row autocorr 0.9995 vs 0.996; and PER-ROW match within ~0.5px (repro [-0.3,-2.2,-2.9,-3.8,-2.1,...] vs measured [-1.0,-2.5,-2.9,-4.1,-2.1,...]). This BIT-LEVEL validates the waviness = verified-dSFMT(seed=1) cumulative walk.
dsfmt_validation.conclusion: BadTV waviness is FAITHFULLY REPRODUCIBLE with the existing verified dSFMT at t=0. The engine badtv.ts currently GATES IT OFF (wavy=null). Wiring the dSFMT walk into the waviness path (behind an env flag) would make BadTV node-boundary matchable at t=0 for the deterministic waviness+scanline+saturate path (Static=0).
